rss_articles.summary: Dafeng Lin, Junmin Shi and Weitong Hu have been charged with 44 offenses in total.

rss_articles.summary: The privacy of phone users is under attack, as proven with SS7 and Diameter signaling data examined by The Citizen Lab.

rss_articles.title: Wacky Call Increments Reveal Deeper Issues with Restoring Trust
rss_articles.summary: Complicated changes to wholesale prices are being communicated with minimal notice.

rss_articles.title: The npm Threat Landscape: Attack Surface and Mitigations
rss_articles.summary: Unit 42 analyzes npm supply chain evolution post-Shai Hulud. Discover wormable malware, CI/CD persistence, multi-stage attacks and more. The post The npm Threat Landscape: Attack Surface and Mitigations appeared first on Unit 42.

rss_articles.title: TGR-STA-1030: New Activity in Central and South America
rss_articles.summary: Unit 42 research reports that TGR-STA-1030 remains an active threat, particularly in Central and South America. The post TGR-STA-1030: New Activity in Central and South America appeared first on Unit 42.

rss_articles.title: Frontier AI and the Future of Defense: Your Top Questions Answered
rss_articles.summary: What are the next steps for security leaders in this new age of frontier AI? We answer the top 10 questions customers are asking. The post Frontier AI and the Future of Defense: Your Top Questions Answered appeared first on Unit 42.

rss_articles.summary: Unit 42 reveals how multi-agent AI systems can autonomously attack cloud environments. Learn critical insights and vital lessons for proactive security. rss_articles.title: When Wi-Fi Encryption Fails: Protecting Your Enterprise from AirSnitch Attacks
rss_articles.summary: Unit 42 research reveals AirSnitch attacks bypass WPA2/3 Wi-Fi encryption and client isolation, exposing critical infrastructure vulnerabilities. The post When Wi-Fi Encryption Fails: Protecting Your Enterprise from AirSnitch Attacks appeared first on Unit 42.

rss_articles.title: FTC Takes Action Against High-Level MLM Participants who Deceived Workers About the Amount of Money They Can Earn
rss_articles.summary: FTC order settling the agency’s allegations will prohibit Steven and Gina Merritt from making deceptive earnings claims The Federal Trade Commission is taking action against high-level participants in a multilevel marketing (MLM) company over allegations they used false or baseless earning claims to recruit workers, most of whom did not earn any money.View Press Release

rss_articles.title: New FTC Data Show People Have Lost Billions to Social Media Scams
rss_articles.summary: Social media was the costliest fraud contact method in 2025 and reported losses increased eightfold since 2020 New data from the Federal Trade Commission show that, in 2025, nearly 30% of people who reported losing money to a scam said that it started on social media, with reported losses reaching a staggering $2.1 billion.View Press Release

rss_articles.title: FTC Charts Path to Restore Competition in Texas Anesthesia Markets in USAP Litigation
rss_articles.summary: The Federal Trade Commission has reached an agreement in principle with U.S. Anesthesia Partners Inc. (USAP) to settle the litigation and restore competition for anesthesia services in Texas.View Press Release

rss_articles.title: FTC Sues to Stop Deceptive Health Care Scheme
rss_articles.summary: FTC alleges scheme has generated millions of dollars by deceiving consumers seeking comprehensive health plans or those who already have coverage At the Federal Trade Commission’s request, a U.S.View Press Release

rss_articles.title: Supreme Court signals location data searches should require a warrant
rss_articles.summary: Privacy advocates had worried that the high court would rule that geofencing does not qualify as a constitutionally protected search, opening the door to much broader use of warrantless reverse searches of all types.

rss_articles.title: Tennessee becomes second state to ban cryptocurrency ATMs over scam concerns
rss_articles.summary: State officials said they observed overseas criminals carrying out government impersonation or tech support cons, as well as romance and pig butchering scams using cryptocurrency ATMs.

rss_articles.title: Money launderer for crypto thieves given 5-year sentence
rss_articles.summary: A California man was sentenced to more than five years in prison for his role in supporting a cybercriminal organization that stole about $260 million worth of cryptocurrency from victims.

rss_articles.title: Disinformation campaign targeted Tibetan parliament-in-exile elections
rss_articles.summary: The operation, identified by the Digital Forensic Research Lab (DFRLab), was part of Spamouflage, a long-running influence network linked to Beijing.

rss_articles.title: Italy extradites alleged Chinese state hacker to US
rss_articles.summary: A Chinese national accused of being a member of a state-backed hacking group that allegedly broke into systems to steal COVID-19 vaccine information has been extradited to the U.S. from Milan.

rss_articles.title: Microsoft asks iPhone users to reauthenticate after Outlook outage
rss_articles.summary: After addressing a widespread outage that affected Outlook.com users worldwide on Monday, Microsoft has asked iPhone users to re-enter their credentials to regain access to their Outlook and Hotmail accounts via the default Mail app. [...]

rss_articles.title: Robinhood account creation flaw abused to send phishing emails
rss_articles.summary: Online trading platform Robinhood's account creation process was exploited by threat actors to inject phishing messages into legitimate emails, tricking users into believing their accounts had suspicious activity. [...]

rss_articles.title: GlassWorm malware attacks return via 73 OpenVSX "sleeper" extensions
rss_articles.summary: A new wave of the Glassworm campaign is targeting the OpenVSX ecosystem with 73 "sleeper" extensions that turn malicious after an update. [...]

rss_articles.title: Canada arrests three for operating “SMS blaster” device in Toronto
rss_articles.summary: Canadian authorities have arrested three men for operating an "SMS blaster" device that pretends to be a cellular tower to send phishing texts to nearby phones. [...]

rss_articles.title: Alleged Silk Typhoon hacker extradited to US for cyberespionage
rss_articles.summary: A Chinese national accused of carrying out cyberespionage operations for China's intelligence services has been extradited from Italy to the United States to face criminal charges. [...]

rss_articles.title: FTC: Americans lost over $2.1 billion to social media scams in 2025
rss_articles.summary: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) warned of a massive increase in losses from social media scams since 2020, exceeding $2.1 billion in 2025. [...]

rss_articles.title: PyPI package with 1.1M monthly downloads hacked to push infostealer
rss_articles.summary: An attacker pushed a malicious version of the popular elementary-data package Python Package Index (PyPI) to steal sensitive developer data and cryptocurrency wallets. [...]

rss_articles.title: Home security giant ADT data breach affects 5.5 million people
rss_articles.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion group stole the personal information of 5.5 million individuals after breaching the systems of home security giant ADT earlier this month, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]

rss_articles.summary: On Thursday, April 30 at 2:00 PM ET, BleepingComputer will host a live webinar with threat intelligence company Flare and threat intelligence researcher Tammy Harper, exploring how security teams can identify early warning signs of attacks before they escalate into incidents. rss_articles.title: Medtronic confirms breach after hackers claim 9 million records theft
rss_articles.summary: Medical device giant Medtronic disclosed last week that hackers breached its network and accessed data in "certain corporate IT systems." [...]

rss_articles.title: Money launderer linked to $230M crypto heist gets 70 months in prison
rss_articles.summary: ​22-year-old Evan Tangeman of Newport Beach, California, was sentenced to 70 months in prison for laundering funds stolen in a massive $230 million cryptocurrency heist. [...]

rss_articles.title: Deepfake Voice Attacks are Outpacing Defenses: What Security Leaders Should Know
rss_articles.summary: Three seconds of audio is all it takes to clone a voice for fraud. Adaptive Security shows how deepfake calls trick employees into sending real money—and why most defenses don't catch them. [...]

rss_articles.title: Microsoft says Outlook.com outage is causing sign‑in failures
rss_articles.summary: Microsoft is investigating an ongoing Outlook.com outage that is causing intermittent signing issues and preventing customers from accessing their mailboxes. [...]

rss_articles.title: American utility firm Itron discloses breach of internal IT network
rss_articles.summary: Itron, Inc. has disclosed, via an 8-K filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), a cybersecurity incident in which an unauthorized third party accessed certain internal systems. [...]

rss_articles.title: Microsoft rolls out revamped Windows Insider Program
rss_articles.summary: Microsoft says it's rolling out a revamped Windows Insider Program experience as part of the broader plans to address performance and reliability concerns affecting Windows 11. [...]

rss_articles.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
rss_articles.summary: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.
